signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.55 — Strait of Hormuz closure pushes WTI crude +2.78% to $101.6, driving USD bid via energy-import inflation risk for Japan. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% (+300bp) carry sustains the floor despite Iran tensions. JPY COT LF net short -61,340 (36.0% long) remains crowded; intervention risk rises on a sustained break toward 160.00. Carry dominates until VIX > 25.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (specs at 18.5% long extreme) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for 27 May. Speculators are aggressively fading a hawkish central bank while NZD/USD holds at 0.5952. If RBNZ delivers a hike or hawkish guidance, short-squeeze risk is elevated as crowded spec shorts unwind. Watch for 0.6000 resistance test.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 113.827 — G8 spread of 0.807% confirms a bilateral theme as AUD outperforms (+0.346%) and JPY lags (-0.461%). RBA 4.35% vs BoJ 0.75% (+360bp) carry advantage drives the divergence. Retail 84% short AUD/JPY diverges from COT LF net long +58,994, suggesting retail is unsuccessfully fading institutional carry flows. Watch for retail capitulation if 114.00 breaks.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Political Risk + Yield Surge
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3541 — UK 10Y yields surge to 4.97% as markets price 'Zombie Government' risk following ministerial resignations. Despite BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% (0bp) neutral carry, GBP is underperforming peers as political instability offsets the yield spike. COT LF net long +31,244 (65.3% long) faces liquidation risk if 1.3500 support fails. Watch for US CPI at 12:30 UTC for USD-side volatility.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Retail Sentiment Extreme
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.781 — Retail 86% long vs 14% short represents an extreme contrarian reversal risk. This retail positioning diverges from COT LF net short -7,392 (30.5% long), where institutions remain bearish. Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0% (+375bp) carry supports USD, but retail crowding suggests upside exhaustion. Watch for a break below 0.7780 to confirm retail washout.
